138.reason: [When pos reaches input.length() - 1, this branch sets curChar to '\0' and increments pos to input.length(). However, if getChar() is called again when pos is already input.length(), neither condition (pos < input.length() - 1 nor pos == input.length() - 1) is true, so curChar remains '\0' and pos is not updated. This causes the lexer to incorrectly treat the end-of-file marker as a valid character, leading to the failure to recognize "getint" as a reserved word because the lexer continues processing with curChar = '\0' instead of properly terminating.]
